title: Diana Obeid

published_department: Modern and Classical Languages and Literatures

organizational_affiliations.label: Modern and Classical Languages and Literatures

biography: Diana Obeid is a Lecturer of Arabic and Middle Eastern Studies and co-Director of the Middle East and North Africa Studies Program.
education_entries: DLS in Liberal Studies, Georgetown University
MA in Humanities, Old Dominion University
BA in English Literature, Lebanese University
research_interests: Prison Literature, Women and gender issues in the Arab World, translation studies, war narratives, and comparative Literature.
teaching_interests: Arabic language /Modern Arabic Literature/ Prison writings
publications: Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New Arab Female Prison Discourse . Bloomsbury.
Book, Scholarly-New Political Prison Discourse in the Arab World: An Analysis of Selected Writings from Egypt and Syria . Bloomsbury.
Book, Scholarly-New Ink and Identity: Unraveling Discourse and Gender in Arab Women’s Writings . Bloomsbury Publishing.
Book, Scholarly-New Nawal al-Saadawi and Hanan al-Shaykh's Authorship: Between Arab and Western Reception . Lexington Books.

Presentations: Diana Obeid, FLAVA, "The Flipped Classroom," Washington and Lee University. (2019)
Diana Obeid, Alpha Mu Gamma 36th National Convention, "Arab Women in Prisons," Mount St Vincent, NY. (2018)
